methods.description: Process for collecting sediment cores and preparing samples from Site U1443 for analysis, including discrete samples for isotope analysis and U-channels for XRF scanning.

steps.description: Samples used in this study are from Site U1443 (5 • 23.01 N, 90 • 21.71 E, water depth 2924 m), drilled during IODP Expedition 353... At Site U1443, the use of Advanced Piston Coring (APC) and half-length APC drilling techniques down to > 200 m CSF... allowed recovery of a compete, spliced Neogene sedimentary section spanning 0-195 m CCSF...
steps.searchable_summary: Drill sediment cores using APC and half-length APC techniques at Site U1443

steps.description: Late Miocene records cover the interval 70.06 m CCSF (U1443B 7H 5W 75-76 cm) to 122.76 m CCSF (U1443C 15H 4W 148-149 cm), following the revised shipboard splice... Cores were sampled (1 cm half rounds) at a depth resolution of 4 cm in the upper part of the late Miocene interval (70.06-114.18 m) and 2 cm in the lower part (114.18-122.76 m)...
steps.searchable_summary: Sample sediment cores at specified depth intervals and resolutions for discrete analysis
inputs: Spliced section depth range (70.06-122.76 m CCSF)
outputs: Discrete sediment samples (1 cm half rounds)

steps.description: U-channels for XRF scanning were sampled from archive halves of sediment cores at Kochi Core Centre (Japan) during the post-cruise sampling party for 39 sections included in the splice...
steps.searchable_summary: Sample U-channels from archive halves of sediment cores for XRF scanning

methods.description: Procedures used to establish a chronological framework for the sediment core samples, including biostratigraphy, magnetostratigraphy, and astronomical tuning, and validating the resulting age model using spectral analysis and comparison with independent records.

steps.description: Seven calcareous nannofossil bio-events dated between 9.53 and 5.04 Ma were identified at ∼ 0.5 to 1 m resolution in Site U1443 splice samples...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify calcareous nannofossil bio-events in sediment samples
outputs: List of identified bio-events with depths and preliminary ages

steps.description: To check for orbital periodicities prior to tuning, wavelet analyses were performed on benthic isotope records in the depth domain and on the revised nannofossil-based age model (using a fourth-order polynomial fit, Fig All time series were first interpolated to constant depth or age resolution, such that the maximum resolution present was preserved (2 cm and 2 kyr for benthic isotope records in the depth and age domain, respectively). Records were then detrended to remove signals with periods longer than one-third of the length of the dataset using the "bandpass" function in the R package Astrochron (Meyers, 2014).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform wavelet analysis on benthic isotope records and age model after interpolation and detrending
inputs: Benthic isotope records (δ18O, δ13C) in depth domain

steps.description: Using revised nannofossil datums (Table S2, Fig. S1) and shipboard magnetostratigraphy as preliminary age-depth tie points ( Fig. 3), an astronomical age model was constructed by tuning our monospecific benthic δ 18 O record to an eccentricity plus tilt (ET) target curve (Laskar et al., 2004) (Table S3, Fig. 4). We used a minimal tuning approach, tying ET maxima to benthic δ 18 O minima, with at most one tie point per ∼ 100 kyr and often one every 200-300 kyr...
steps.searchable_summary: Construct astronomical age model by tuning benthic δ18O record to an ET target curve using biostratigraphic and magnetostratigraphic tie points
inputs: Eccentricity plus tilt (ET) target curve (Laskar et al., 2004)
Tuning parameters (minimal tuning, tie point criteria)

steps.description: Both wavelet analyses (Fig. 4f, g) and spectral analyses (Fig. S2e, f) of the tuned benthic records reveal significant (> 99 %) orbital periodicities of ∼ 405 and 41 kyr for δ 18 O and ∼ 405, 125, 95, 53, and 41 kyr for δ 13 C...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform wavelet and spectral analyses on tuned benthic isotope records
outputs: Spectral analysis results (identified periodicities, significance levels)

steps.description: Cross-spectral analysis between δ 18 O and δ 13 C reveals > 95 % coherency in the 41 and 405 kyr bands (Fig. S4a).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform cross-spectral analysis between tuned benthic δ18O and δ13C records

steps.description: Our age model is supported by close agreement between Site U1443 benthic δ 18 O and δ 13 C data and independent orbitally tuned benthic isotope records from the South China Sea (ODP 1146) , equatorial Pacific (IODP Sites U1338 and 1337)... and equatorial Atlantic (ODP Sites 926 and 999)... (Figs. S5, S6).
steps.searchable_summary: Validate age model by comparing U1443 benthic isotope data and age model with independent records from other sites
inputs: Site U1443 tuned benthic δ18O and δ13C records
Independent orbitally tuned benthic isotope records and age models from other sites (e.g., ODP 1146, U1338, U1337, ODP 926, 999)

steps.description: Mean temporal resolution of the Site U1443 benthic isotope record is 4.2 kyr. Between 9 and 7.6 Ma, mean benthic δ 18 O values vary between 2.5 ‰ and 2.8 ‰... Between 7.6 and 6.7 Ma, mean values vary between 0.7 ‰ and 1.1 ‰... then decrease from ∼ 1 ‰ to −0.2 ‰ between 7.6 and 6.7 Ma, reflecting the globally recognized Late Miocene Carbon Isotope Shift (LMCIS)...
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze mean values, trends, and features (e.g., LMCIS) in tuned benthic isotope records
outputs: Identified trends and features (e.g., LMCIS timing, magnitude)

steps.description: All δ 18 O records are based on Cibicidoides wuellerstorfi or C. mundulus; therefore no corrections are applied, following Jöhnck et al. (2021). The Site 926 record includes δ 13 C corrections for some samples due to the multispecific nature of the record (Drury et al., 2017). No correction was applied to C. wuellerstorfi or C. mundulus δ 13 C values.
steps.searchable_summary: Apply species-specific corrections to benthic isotope data if necessary
outputs: Corrected benthic isotope data (or confirmation of no correction needed)

methods.description: Procedures for processing raw XRF scanning data, calibrating elemental counts, calculating derived parameters like CaCO3 content, and analyzing elemental trends, including validation of derived parameters.

steps.description: Scanning XRF results are shown in Fig. 6. Raw and calibrated elemental data show consistent trends and amplitude variability (Fig. 6).
inputs: U-channel samples (from Sediment Core Sampling method)
outputs: Raw XRF counts-per-second data for various elements

steps.description: For Ti, Ba, Al, and Mn, the re-scaled counts-per-second values in the 113.37 to 122.76 m CCSF interval (Lübbers et al., 2019) fell outside of our calibration range; thus data below 113.6 m (∼ 8.15 Ma) were not converted to concentrations (Fig. 6).
steps.searchable_summary: Calibrate raw XRF counts-per-second to elemental concentrations, noting calibration range limitations
outputs: Calibrated elemental concentration data (or re-scaled counts where calibration is not possible)

steps.description: Confidence in our method of calculating CaCO 3 content is provided by very good agreement with independent %CaCO 3 estimates for the middle and early late Miocene interval of Site U1443 based on calibration of XRF-derived counts of (Ca/ (Ca, Al, Si, K, Ti, Mn, Fe, S)) to discrete CaCO 3 measurements (Lübbers et al., 2019)...
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate CaCO3 content from XRF-derived elemental counts using a specified ratio
inputs: Calibrated elemental concentration data (or re-scaled counts) for Ca, Al, Si, K, Ti, Mn, Fe, S
Calculation formula (Ca / (Ca, Al, Si, K, Ti, Mn, Fe, S))

steps.description: In brief, Al, Si, Ti, Fe, Rb, and K show similar trends... Ca and Ba show a minor long-term decrease... while Sr and Mn increase... All elements show high-frequency variability throughout.
steps.searchable_summary: Analyze trends and variability in calibrated elemental concentrations and calculated CaCO3 content

steps.description: Confidence in our method of calculating CaCO 3 content is provided by very good agreement with independent %CaCO 3 estimates...
steps.searchable_summary: Validate calculated CaCO3 content by comparing with independent estimates
inputs: Independent CaCO3 estimates (e.g., from discrete measurements or other calibrations)
